prompt: Construct a LabOps Resource & Settlement Portal supporting offline lab operations, internal fulfillment, and accountable billing across multiple roles. Regular Users request lab access and book resources (labs, rooms, workstations, equipment, and consumables) from a responsive Livewire interface that adapts cleanly to desktop, tablet, and mobile, with keyboard-only navigation and readable contrast defaults. Staff members sign in to manage their profiles and daily workload, including completion of required employment details (employee ID, department, title) before they can approve reservations or perform check-in/check-out. Community Group-Leaders represent defined service areas (for example, a corporate campus, medical plaza, or industrial park) and can be bound to specific locations with commission eligibility; their dashboard shows attributed orders and performance totals for a selected date range. Administrators configure sellable offerings mapped to service areas and define pricing rules by time slot, headcount, member tier, package, and coupon, with clear upfront totals including sales tax where applicable. The ordering flow provides immediate UI feedback on availability, price breakdowns, coupon acceptance, and conflicts; images and attachments use compression and lazy loading to meet core KPIs such as first meaningful interaction under 2.5 seconds on a standard laptop. Users can view order status, cancellations, and refund outcomes in an order history screen, while staff and group-leaders can view settlement summaries and export them for internal accounting. The backend uses Laravel to expose REST-style endpoints consumed by Livewire actions, enforcing a decoupled API authorization layer with RBAC and button-level feature control per role (User, Staff, Group-Leader, Admin). Because the deployment must run fully offline, all authentication is local username/password with JWT-based sessions, configurable to expire after 30 minutes of inactivity and refreshable within a 7-day max session window; concurrent sessions are limited to two devices per account with an admin override to revoke tokens. Passwords must be at least 12 characters with complexity rules and are hashed; recovery is handled via offline admin reset with audit logging, not email/SMS. PostgreSQL persists hierarchical resource models, status transitions (available, reserved, in use, maintenance, decommissioned), custom attributes, attachment metadata (size/type checks and SHA-256 fingerprinting), and immutable change history. The settlement engine generates order, refund, reconciliation, and group-leader commission records using deterministic rules: cancellations within 15 minutes are fully refundable, later cancellations default to a 20% fee unless the resource is marked unavailable by staff. Group-leader settlement cycles are configurable (weekly or biweekly) with a 3-business-day hold for dispute resolution, and exports are produced locally as CSV/PDF without external services. Sensitive fields such as phone numbers are optional, stored encrypted at rest, and masked in non-admin views.
